span.p: Coarse — open the item at all, or fine — which rows/columns inside?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Item permissions / sharing on the DATA plane
NOT workspace roles — those are control-plane, not data access

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: How does the consumer read the data?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SQL-native security IN the warehouse
RLS security policy + predicate · CLS GRANT/DENY · DDM=display only
(warehouse-security BP)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Semantic-model RLS / OLS
(Power BI roles)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
OneLake data-access roles
folder/table-grain on the lake plane

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Also consumed via a Direct Lake semantic model?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Pick ONE canonical RLS layer — NOT both
warehouse RLS can force Direct Lake to DirectQuery fallback

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Enforce in the single plane the consumer uses

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: True access control, or just display masking?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
DDM masks display — NOT access control
never rely on it where CLS/RLS is the real requirement
